summary: PASS: revision-28 authority, context, privacy, rendering, handoff, media, proxy/accessor, package, fan-in, and trace boundaries fail closed at the reviewed core boundary; runtime enforcement remains external.

artifacts.name: security-trace-review.md
artifacts.content:
# Security and Trace Review

Outcome: PASS

## Exact Bindings

- Reviewed goal revision 28, compilation `sha256:18e40b2586375f0b7004fe088b7b2ebc2f0bd607dec27963c1b68c7ee719df7c`, and blueprint `sha256:c6ec74a394982b8c9919c8a6261b3440bf996a3d63e363f6e54f93c998bcf30d`.
- Independently verified all 49 declared review-context files against their raw SHA-256 digests and byte counts: 49 passed, zero failed, totaling 751444 bytes.
- Verified the dependency handoff as exactly 12808 bytes with raw digest `sha256:af187af6a16ac6d7166d1e690b125bb005131e6f57423a7e36e0320373b28b1d` and independently reproduced semantic digest `sha256:8628d3622c1b50f05b6bf4b3e75a0feb33e2c30faf38f5d75f402b4a2b43cab7`.
- Independently reproduced the fan-in assessment content digest `sha256:fdf985ba408cbcc9149636f2a5b1864f31fc2aefc84268b98d3ce9ff02449fb7`. It targets this exact agent, contains the exact required dependency once, reports no missing dependency, binds package `sha256:7dd562d20096778abcd15601edc4226064865cf418c33f07f8e8ad25878faba0`, and has `integrationReady: true`.

## Boundary Review

- Authority and context fail closed. Hostile inputs are detached through plain-JSON snapshots; closed schemas reject undeclared provider, runtime, grant, role, and credential fields; repository locators reject unsafe segments; fragment-free paths must be covered by declared read scopes; work-unit scopes and permission demands must exactly narrow the owner ceiling; compiled blueprints contain only used modules, capabilities, context, scopes, permissions, evidence duties, dependencies, handoff fields, and stop conditions.
- Privacy and normalized controls fail closed. Compiler and handoff strings, including property keys, are checked before launchable values are returned. High-confidence secret patterns, malformed surrogate text, C0/C1 controls, DEL, and bidi formatting controls are rejected. Handoff artifact content permits LF only; TAB, CR, CRLF, other unsafe controls, malformed Unicode, and secret-bearing content are rejected. Public diagnostics suppress unsafe artifacts and retain only safe pointer prefixes.
- Rendering and package verification fail closed. Compilation is reconstructed from normalized source semantics before rendering. Untrusted values remain inside dynamically sized Markdown fences, while generated paths use digest-derived identifiers. Manifest and package identities bind ordered paths, media types, byte counts, raw file digests, blueprint digests, and compilation identity. Package verification snapshots hostile values, reconstructs the single compilation file, rerenders the complete package, requires canonical equality, and checks both externally retained expected digests. Coordinated rewrites cannot retain the approved package identity absent a hash break.
- Raw handoff and deterministic media boundaries fail closed. Verification copies accepted byte arrays, uses fatal UTF-8 and duplicate-key-aware JSON parsing, validates the closed handoff schema, and binds goal, compilation, blueprint, destination, owned work, exact artifact names, deterministic media types, and every evidence duty. `.json`, `.md`, and other artifact names deterministically map to `application/json`, `text/markdown`, and `text/plain`. Accepted results preserve raw, semantic, and per-artifact digests.
- Proxy and accessor boundaries fail closed for compiler, renderer, package expectation, raw handoff, and fan-in inputs. Proxies are rejected before reflective traversal, and array/object accessors are rejected from descriptors without invoking caller code.
- Fan-in and trace boundaries fail closed. The assessor derives the exact transitive dependency closure, rejects duplicate, unknown, malformed, substituted, and outside-closure handoffs, reports missing or non-pass dependencies as not ready, and permits readiness only when every exact dependency has one verified pass handoff. Prelaunch receipt and authorization logic binds both package pairs, raw receipt and handoff paths, bytes, digests, outcomes, duplicate reviewer identities, and the independent review duties.
- Package schema availability is closed and offline. `package.json` exports common, specialist-compiler, and specialist-handoff schemas through supported subpaths; loaders use only hardcoded shipped schema resources; installed-consumer sources retain the same independent two-digest expectation and generated media contract in JavaScript and TypeScript hosts.
- V11 does not claim runtime enforcement. Documentation and generated contracts consistently leave context delivery, isolation, permission enforcement, execution, approval custody, persistence, merge, and memory effects to an external host.

## Verification Limits

Regression sources covering authority, Unicode, privacy, rendering, tamper resistance, schema exports, handoffs, media substitution, proxy/accessor rejection, fan-in, prelaunch authorization, and packed-consumer parity were inspected. No build or test command was executed because this review lane is read-only and those gates may materialize artifacts.

No release-blocking security or trace finding was identified, and none of the three explicit stop conditions was reached.

assumptions: The host delivered the generated contract and machine fan-in assessment from the approved revision-28 package; this review validates their internal and cryptographic bindings but does not attest host custody or sandbox enforcement.
risks: Filesystem isolation, permission enforcement, runtime ordering, approval custody, and evidence persistence remain external host responsibilities.
No build or test command was executed during this read-only lane; release-gate execution is assigned to other evidence duties.
followUps: Preserve the exact raw dependency handoff and fan-in assessment with the approved digest pair, then reconstruct and verify the package again after integration.
